name: pycentroid
documentation:
# pycentroid

![pycentroid logo](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/131147072?s=200&v=4)

@themost-framework for Python

[@themost-framework](https://github.com/themost-framework) is a fully-featured end-to-end framework for building scalable data-driven web applications and services under node.js. It consists of a wide set of server-side libraries and client-side tools for helping developers creating scalable and configurable production environments. `pycentroid` is a `@themost-framework` alternative for python.

## pycentroid.client

A client-side library of [@themost-framework](https://github.com/themost-framework) application for python

```python
from typing import List
from pycentroid.client import ClientDataContext, ClientContextOptions
from pycentroid.query import select

async def get_products():
    context = ClientDataContext(ClientContextOptions('http://localhost:3000/api/'))
    # get products
    items: List = await context.model('Products').as_queryable().select(
            lambda x: select(id=x.id, name=x.name, product_model=x.model,)
        ).where(
            lambda x: x.category == 'Laptops'
        ).get_items()
    return items
```

Read more at [pycentroid.client](./pycentroid/client/README.md)

## pycentroid.query

A database-agnostic query module which for writing SQL expressions of any kind

```python
from pycentroid.query import QueryExpression, QueryEntity, SqlFormatter, select


products = QueryEntity('ProductData')
query = QueryExpression(products).select(
        lambda x: select(id=x.id, name=x.name, price=round(x.price, 2))
    ).where(
        lambda x: round(x.price, 2) < 800 and x.category == 'Laptops'
    )
sql = SqlFormatter().format(query)
# sql > SELECT id,name,ROUND(price,2) AS price FROM ProductData WHERE ((ROUND(price,2)<800) AND (category='Laptops'))
```

## pycentroid.data

A next-generation ORM data module for developing data-driven application and services.

```python
from pycentroid.data.application import DataApplication
from os.path import abspath, join, dirname
APP_PATH = abspath(join(dirname(__file__), '..'))


async def main():
    app = DataApplication(cwd=APP_PATH)
    context = app.create_context()
    results = await context.model('Order').where(
        lambda x: x.orderedItem.category == 'Desktops'
        ).get_items()
    print(results)
```

methods.name: pycentroid.query.utils.SqlUtils.escape
documentation:
Escapes any value to an equivalent sql string

Args:
    value (*): A value to escape
    timezone (*): A value to escape

Returns:
        value (str) An escaped sql string

methods.name: pycentroid.query.utils.SqlUtils.convert_timezone
documentation:
Converts a timezone to a time offset e.g. -120, +60 in minutes

Args:
    tz (str): A string which represents a timezone

Returns:
        (int) An integer which represents the time offset

methods.name: pycentroid.query.utils.SqlUtils.bytes_to_string
documentation:
Converts a bytearray to an equivalent hex string

Args:
    value (bytearray): The array of bytes to convert

Raises:
    TypeError: Expected a valid bytearray

Returns:
    str: The equivalent hex string

methods.name: pycentroid.query.utils.SqlUtils.dict_to_values
documentation:
Converts a dictionary object to an equivalent sequence of SQL fields values

Args:
    value (dict): The dictionary object to convert
    timezone (str, optional): An optional timezone expression to use while converting datetime values

Raises:
    TypeError: Expected a valid dictionary

Returns:
    str: The equivalent SQL expression

methods.name: pycentroid.query.utils.SqlUtils.object_to_values
documentation:
Converts an object to an equivalent sequence of SQL fields values

Args:
    value (obj): The object to convert
    timezone (str, optional): An optional timezone expression to use while converting datetime values

Raises:
    TypeError: Expected a valid object

Returns:
    str: The equivalent SQL expression

methods.name: pycentroid.query.query_expression.QueryExpression.left_join
documentation:
Prepares a left join expression with the given collection

Args:
    collection (str): The collection to join
    local_field (str): Specifies the local field in join expression
    foreign_field (str): Specifies the foreign field in join expression
    alias (str, optional): Specifies the alias of the given collection in join expression. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    QueryExpression

methods.name: pycentroid.query.query_expression.QueryExpression.right_join

Args:

    local_field (str): Specifies the local field in join expression
    foreign_field (str): Specifies the foreign field in join expression
    alias (str, optional): Specifies the alias of the given collection in join expression. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    QueryExpression

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataObjectPrivilege.mask
documentation: A number which represents permission mask (1=Read, 2=Create, 4=Update, 8=Delete, 16=Execute)

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataObjectPrivilege.type
documentation: A string which represents the permission scope.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataObjectPrivilege.account
documentation:
A string which represents the name of the security group
where this privilege will be applied e.g. Administrators, Sales etc.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataObjectPrivilege.filter
documentation:
A string which represents a filter expression for this privilege.
This attribute is used for self privileges which are commonly derived from user's attributes
e.g. 'owner eq me()' or 'orderStatus eq 1 and customer eq me()' etc.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelConstraint.type
documentation: A string which represents the type of this constraint e.g. unique

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelConstraint.description
documentation:
A short description for this constraint
e.g. Unique identifier field must be unique across different records.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.name
documentation: A string which represents the name of this attribute e.g. title, description, dateCreated etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.description
documentation: A string which represents a short description of this attribute

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.title
documentation: A string which represents the name of this attribute e.g. title, description, dateCreated etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.type
documentation: A string which represents the type of this attribute e.g. Counter, Integer, Number, Text etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.size
documentation: A number which represents the maximum size for this attribute e.g. the size of a text field etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.scale
documentation: A number which represents the number of digits of a decimal number

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.nullable
documentation: A boolean which indicates whether this attribute is nullable or not.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.editable
documentation: A boolean which indicates whether this attribute is editable or not.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.readonly
documentation:
A boolean which indicates whether this attribute is readonly or not.
A readonly value must have a default value or a calculated value.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.primary
documentation: A boolean which indicates whether this attribute is a key column or not.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.indexed
documentation: A boolean which indicates whether this attribute is an indexed column or not.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.property
documentation:
A string which optionally represents the name of this attribute in object mapping.
This name may defer from the name of the database field.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.many
documentation:
A boolean value which indicates whether this attribute
represents a one-to-many or many-to-many association between two models.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.multiplicity
documentation: A string which defines the multiplicity level of an association between two objects

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.expandable
documentation: A boolean value which indicates whether the associated object(s) will be automatically expanded or not.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataField.nested
documentation:
A boolean which indicates whether this attribute defines an association between two models
where child objects are always treated as a part of the parent object.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelEventListener.name
documentation: A string which the name of this event listener e.g. 'After Update Person'

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelEventListener.type
documentation: A string which represents the path of the module that exports this listener.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.name
documentation: A string which represents the name of this model e.g. Order, Customer, Person etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.title
documentation: A string which represents the title of this e.g. Supplier Orders, Person Followers etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.inherits
documentation:
A string which represents the model which is inherited by this model
e.g. User inherits Account, Person inherits Party etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.implements
documentation:
A string which represents the model which is implemented by this model
e.g. ActionStatusType model implements Enumeration model etc

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.sealed
documentation: A boolean which indicates whether this model is being upgraded automatically or not.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.hidden
documentation: A boolean which indicates whether this model is hidden or not. The default value is false.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.classPath
documentation: A string which represents a module path that exports a class which maps this database model

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.version
documentation: The version of this item

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.source
documentation: A string which holds the database object of this model.

class_variables.name: pycentroid.data.types.DataModelProperties.view
documentation: A string which holds the database object that is going to be used while fetching data.
